goal: Find stronger architecture, RAG, tokenizer, and encoder directions for AVA under a 4 GB single-GPU constraint and validate the cheapest concrete branches locally.

papers.title: Transformers are SSMs: Generalized Models and Efficient Algorithms Through Structured State Space Duality
papers.takeaway: State-space / attention hybrids are credible small-model efficiency candidates when KV cache or long context becomes the bottleneck.

papers.title: Hymba: A Hybrid-head Architecture for Small Language Models
papers.takeaway: For small models, mixing attention and SSM heads inside the same layer is a serious architecture candidate, not a curiosity.

papers.title: LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation
papers.takeaway: Dual-level graph plus text retrieval is a better long-term memory design than flat nearest-neighbor lookup.

papers.title: From RAG to Memory: Non-Parametric Continual Learning for Large Language Models
papers.takeaway: RAG should evolve into structured memory with associative retrieval, not remain prompt stuffing forever.

papers.title: FlashRAG: A Modular Toolkit for Efficient Retrieval-Augmented Generation Research
papers.takeaway: FlashRAG is the best external research/eval framework reference; it is useful for reproducing RAG baselines, not for AVA runtime itself.

papers.title: FOCUS: Effective Embedding Initialization for Specializing Pretrained Multilingual Models on a Single Language
papers.takeaway: Changing tokenization after pretraining needs smart embedding init; naive remapping is not enough.

papers.title: BGE M3-Embedding: Multi-Lingual, Multi-Functionality, Multi-Granularity Text Embeddings Through Self-Knowledge Distillation
papers.takeaway: If AVA needs a multilingual retrieval encoder later, BGE-M3 is the strongest open reference, but it is heavy for 4 GB local-first use.

papers.title: gte-modernbert-base model card
papers.takeaway: For a local retrieval encoder, gte-modernbert-base is the pragmatic first candidate: 149M parameters, 8192 context, competitive retrieval metrics.

decisions.architecture: AVA-v2 should move away from pure GPT-2 defaults toward a modern dense stack first (RoPE, RMSNorm, SwiGLU, GQA), then test Hymba/Mamba-style hybrid layers after the dense baseline is rebuilt.
decisions.rag_runtime: Adopt the existing hybrid_support_mc branch as the current best public-benchmark retriever. Long-term memory direction should borrow from LightRAG/HippoRAG2, not flat canonical lookup.
decisions.rag_framework: Use FlashRAG as the reference framework for future RAG benchmark reproduction and ablations, not as the product runtime.
decisions.tokenizer: hf_bpe_512 is the current best local tokenizer artifact by compression. However, post-hoc tokenizer migration still collapses the student, so tokenizer changes must happen earlier in training or use a stronger FOCUS-style migration recipe.
decisions.encoder: No external encoder was downloaded yet. The first local candidate is Alibaba-NLP/gte-modernbert-base; the multilingual heavier candidate is BAAI/bge-m3.

broad_support_bank_probe.command: python -m ava.cli benchmark external arc-challenge ... --support-corpus corpora/public_benchmark_distill_v1 --retrieval-mode hybrid_support_mc
broad_support_bank_probe.takeaway: The broader support bank needs indexing or an encoder-backed retriever before it is practical.

next_step: Implement a modern dense AVA-v2 block family and a pluggable encoder-backed hybrid retriever. If a Hugging Face token is available, download gte-modernbert-base first and benchmark it against the current sparse hybrid ARC path.
